scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. _________ / 2024

BETWEEN:

RAM KUMAR, aged 45 years, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru. ... PLAINTIFF

AND:

SURESH PATEL, aged 38 years, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru. ... DEFENDANT

PLAINT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff submits that the Defendant resides within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Koramangala, Bengaluru, and the cause of action arose within Bengaluru, hence this Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction to try this suit.

FACTS OF THE CASE

2. The Plaintiff states that on 15.03.2024, he advanced a hand loan of Rs. 15,00,000 to the Defendant via NEFT bank transfer bearing UTR No. AXIB20240315123456.

3. The Plaintiff submits that the Defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages.

4. The Plaintiff states that the Defendant defaulted on the repayment obligation on 20.06.2024, which is the date from which interest is claimed.

5. The Plaintiff states that he issued a legal notice to the Defendant dated 01.07.2024 demanding repayment.

6. The Plaintiff states that despite the legal notice and repeated oral demands, the Defendant has failed to repay the principal amount.

7. The Plaintiff submits that the right to sue is continuing as the money remains unpaid despite demand.

LEGAL BASIS

8. The Plaintiff relies on {{CONTRACT_ACT_PROVISION}} of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which provides for compensation for loss or damage caused by breach of contract, as the Defendant breached the obligation to repay the loan.

9. The Plaintiff relies on Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, which empowers the Court to award interest on the decretal amount, justifying the claim for interest at 12% per annum.

CAUSE OF ACTION

10. The cause of action arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant defaulted on repayment and continues to subsist as the Defendant has failed to repay the amount despite notice dated 01.07.2024.

LIMITATION

11. The suit is within limitation as per Article 19 of the Limitation Act, 1963, being filed within three years from the date the loan was made on 15.03.2024.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

12. The Plaintiff values the suit for Rs. 15,00,000 towards principal amount and further interest at 12% per annum from 20.06.2024 till realization.

13. The Plaintiff pays court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} as per the Karnataka Court-fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

PRAYER

14. The Plaintiff prays for judgment and decree against the Defendant for:
(a) Rs. 15,00,000 towards principal amount;
(b) Interest at 12% per annum from 20.06.2024 till realization;
(c) Costs of the suit; and
(d) Any other relief this Hon'ble Court deems fit.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

15. The Plaintiff relies on the following documents:
(a) NEFT bank transfer record with UTR: AXIB20240315123456 dated 15.03.2024;
(b) WhatsApp messages acknowledging the loan;
(c) Legal notice dated 01.07.2024.

VERIFICATION

I, Ram Kumar, the Plaintiff herein, do hereby verify and declare that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 15 of the plaint are true and correct to my knowledge and belief, and no part of it is false and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at Bengaluru on this the _________ day of _________, 2024.

PLAINTIFF

ADVOCATE FOR PLAINTIFF
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
{{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT_NUMBER}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
{{ADVOCATE_CONTACT}}

citation_issues.message: 'Section 73 Indian Contract' not in verified_provisions — may be correct but unverified
